verses.commentary: Caesar Augustus issues decree for empire-wide registration (apographesthai, ἀπογράφεσθαι). Roman imperial machinery moves a Galilean couple to Bethlehem — pagan power inadvertently fulfilling prophecy.
First registration while Quirinius governed Syria — dating crux for historians. Luke anchors his account to verifiable political markers.
Everyone goes to their own town. Joseph's ancestral town is Bethlehem. The administrative requirement positions the Messiah's birth precisely where Micah 5:2 placed it.
Joseph goes up from Galilee, from Nazareth, to Judea, to David's town Bethlehem — because he is of the house and lineage of David. Lukan genealogy through Joseph traced via David emphasized.
To register with Mary, betrothed and pregnant. The couple's social precarity (pregnancy before marriage's consummation) traveled with them.
While there, the days for her to give birth were completed. Timing aligns with the census — sovereign overlap.

Firstborn son (prototokos, πρωτότοκος). Wrapped in cloths (esparganosen, ἐσπαργάνωσεν — swaddled), laid in a manger (phatne, φάτνῃ, feeding trough), because no kataluma (κατάλυμα, guest room, not 'inn') for them. Likely a relative's home, common room full, animal area used.
Shepherds in fields, keeping night watch over their flocks. Marginal occupation — shepherds were ritually unreliable witnesses in rabbinic law. The first heralded are the unheard.

Angel of the Lord appears. Glory of the Lord shines around them (echoing Exodus 16:10; Ezekiel 1). Theophany to shepherds. They fear with great fear (ephobethesan phobon megan, ἐφοβήθησαν φόβον μέγαν).
'Do not fear — I bring good news (euangelizomai, εὐαγγελίζομαι) of great joy for all the people.' Universal scope: 'all the people.' Eu-angelion announced to shepherds, not officials.

'Today (semeron, σήμερον) in David's city is born a Savior (soter, σωτήρ), who is Christ the Lord.' Three titles: Savior, Christ, Lord. Title 'Savior' was claimed by Augustus — Luke's quiet counterclaim.
Sign: 'You will find a baby wrapped in cloths, lying in a manger.' The sign is poverty, not splendor. Heaven's identification is humility.
Suddenly a multitude of the heavenly host (stratia ourania, στρατιὰ οὐράνια — heavenly army). Praising God. Military image inverted: angelic army announces peace.
'Glory to God in the highest, on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased (anthropois eudokias, ἀνθρώποις εὐδοκίας).' The Gloria. Glory above, peace below — the bridge spans them.
Angels depart; shepherds say: 'Let us go now to Bethlehem and see this thing (rhema, ῥῆμα, word/event) that has happened.' Faith mobilizes them. Hurry to verify.
They go with haste (speusantes, σπεύσαντες) and find Mary, Joseph, and the baby in the manger. The angelic description is verified.
Having seen — they spread the word about what was told them about this child. The first evangelists are shepherds. The order of proclamation matters.
All who heard wondered (ethaumasan, ἐθαύμασαν) at what the shepherds said. Astonishment at testimony — not yet faith.

Mary kept all these things (synteroi panta ta rhemata, συνετήρει πάντα τὰ ῥήματα), pondering (symballousa, συμβάλλουσα — putting things together) in her heart. Theological reflection initiated. The mother becomes the primary memory-keeper.
Shepherds return, glorifying and praising God. Worshipers depart, witnesses sent. The pattern of encounter-and-praise repeats.
Eighth day — circumcised, named Jesus, as the angel said before conception. Torah-observance from infancy. Name fulfilled.
Days of purification (Leviticus 12) completed — they bring him up to Jerusalem to present him (parastesai, παραστῆσαι) to the Lord. The Holy Family follows the Law in full.
'Every male that opens the womb is called holy to the Lord' (Exodus 13:2, 12). Firstborn consecrated. Jesus the firstborn keeps the Torah he came to fulfill.
Sacrifice: 'pair of doves or two young pigeons' — Leviticus 12:8 provision for the poor. The Messiah's family cannot afford the lamb. Poverty discreetly signaled.
Simeon in Jerusalem — righteous (dikaios, δίκαιος) and devout (eulabes, εὐλαβής). Waiting for the consolation (paraklesin, παράκλησιν) of Israel (Isaiah 40:1). Holy Spirit was upon him.
Revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he saw the Lord's Christ. Personal prophecy held in patience.
Comes into the temple by the Spirit. Parents bring the child Jesus. Spirit-directed encounter.
Takes the child in his arms, blesses God. Holds the Savior in his hands — the literal image of receiving Christ.

Nunc Dimittis begins. 'Now you let your servant depart in peace, Lord, according to your word.' Despoten (Δέσποτα, sovereign master) — slave-master language. Simeon is the master's slave dismissed at duty's end.
'My eyes have seen your salvation (soterion, σωτήριον).' Salvation visible, embodied. The abstract has become concrete.
'Which you have prepared in the sight of all peoples.' Public, universal preparation.
'A light for revelation to the Gentiles, and the glory of your people Israel.' Isaiah 42:6, 49:6 echo. Israel's glory and Gentile light combined.
Father and mother marvel at what is said about him. The wonder is not lost on them despite Gabriel's announcement, the shepherds' visit, the angels' song. Each revelation deepens.

Simeon blesses them, says to Mary: 'This child is set for the fall (ptosin, πτῶσιν) and rising (anastasin, ἀνάστασιν) of many in Israel, and for a sign that is spoken against (semeion antilegomenon, σημεῖον ἀντιλεγόμενον).' Christ as polarizing line.

'A sword (rhomphaia, ῥομφαία — large two-edged blade) will pierce your own soul also — that thoughts (dialogismoi, διαλογισμοί) of many hearts may be revealed.' Mary's suffering predicted; Christ as revealer of hearts.
Prophetess Anna — daughter of Phanuel, tribe of Asher (a northern tribe, often lost to history). Very advanced in years.
Widow until eighty-four. Did not depart from the temple, worshiping with fasting and prayer night and day. Lifelong dedication.
At that very hour she came up; gave thanks to God; spoke about him to all waiting for the redemption (lytrosis, λύτρωσιν) of Jerusalem. Second witness, female and aged.
Performed everything according to the Law of the Lord, returned to Galilee to Nazareth. Lukan emphasis on Torah-keeping.
Child grew, became strong, filled with wisdom; the grace of God was upon him. Compare 1:80 (John's growth). Both children grow under divine favor.
His parents went every year to Jerusalem for Passover. Annual pilgrimage — pious practice.
When he was twelve — bar mitzvah age approaches. Standard pilgrimage continued.
After festival ended, on the return, Jesus stays behind in Jerusalem. His parents do not know.
Supposing him in the caravan, they go a day's journey before searching among relatives and friends. The caravan culture allows the assumption.
Not finding him, they return to Jerusalem searching.
After three days they find him in the temple, sitting among the teachers, listening and asking questions. The boy participates in rabbinic discourse.
All who heard were amazed at his understanding (synesei, συνέσει) and answers (apokrisesin, ἀποκρίσεσιν). Precocious wisdom acknowledged.
Parents astonished. Mother: 'Child, why have you treated us this way? Your father and I have been searching anxiously.' Mary's distress voiced.

'Why were you searching for me? Did you not know I must be in my Father's house (en tois tou patros mou, ἐν τοῖς τοῦ πατρός μου — in the things / house of my Father)?' First recorded words of Jesus. Father-Son consciousness explicit at age twelve.
They did not understand. Even with all prior revelation, the family does not grasp his self-consciousness. Comprehension is delayed for all observers.

Went down with them to Nazareth, was obedient (hypotassomenos, ὑποτασσόμενος, subordinated) to them. The divine Son lived under human parental authority. Mother kept all these things in her heart (refrain from 2:19).
Jesus increased (proekopten, προέκοπτεν) in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man. Comprehensive growth: intellectual, physical, spiritual, social. Hidden years summarized in one verse.
